topic: A person finds out their partner has been learning their native language
raw_output:
[story 1]

The silence in the apartment was suddenly deafening, thick with an unspoken accusation. I was staring at the textbook lying open on the coffee table, the unfamiliar script swimming before my eyes. Every carefully constructed thought I had managed to assemble dissolved into a frustrating fog. I slowly pushed myself up from the sofa, the movement jerky, and walked over to where Liam was sitting at the kitchen counter, his brow furrowed in concentration over a complex conjugation. "What," I managed, my voice coming out strained and unnaturally high, "is all this?" He looked up, startled, a slight flush creeping up his neck. The realization hit me like a physical blow: he wasn't just casually picking up phrases; he was dedicating hours to mastering the nuances of my mother tongue. My jaw tightened so hard I could feel the muscles ache. I turned away abruptly, grabbing my keys with unnecessary force, the metallic click of the door lock sounding like a gunshot in the quiet room.

[story 2]

I stood frozen in the doorway, my hands clenched into fists so tight my nails dug crescents into my palms. The air in the living room felt suddenly too thin to breathe. Across the room, Mark was tracing letters on a notepad, muttering softly to himself, his lips moving in a rhythm that was distinctly, undeniably, my native language. A slow, hot wave washed over my chest, making my breath catch in my throat. I let out a sharp, involuntary exhale, a sound devoid of any warmth. "Stop," I articulated, the word tasting like ash on my tongue. My eyes flicked from his focused expression to the pages he was studying, and a tight, brittle tension coiled in my shoulders. I walked over, not approaching him, but standing close enough that I could feel the heat radiating off him, my gaze unwavering, sharp enough to cut glass. "Why are you doing this?" The question was low, laced with an intensity that made the silence stretch unbearably.

[story 3]

It started as a casual observation—a misplaced phrase here, a sudden, fluent inflection there. But now, the pattern was undeniable. I found myself listening, half-expecting to catch a slip, a moment of hesitation, anything to deny the truth. When I finally confronted him, the words tumbled out in a rapid-fire sequence, a torrent of disbelief and sharp, stinging questions. My hands were shaking; I kept needing to press them against my thighs to ground myself. "You're... you're learning *my* language?" I repeated the words slowly, deliberately, letting the absurdity of the situation hang heavy in the air between us. I watched his face shift—a flicker of panic, then a kind of defensive resignation. I took a deliberate step back, putting space between us, my posture rigid, every line of my body screaming a refusal to accept what I was seeing. The sheer incongruity of it all was overwhelming, leaving a residue of pure, unadulterated agitation simmering just beneath my skin.
